_meta.description: Canonical validated regex patterns for SWE analysis. Precision measured on 50-sample stratified (25 per period) semantic check. Threshold for PASS: >=0.80 conservative precision (AMB treated as non-TP).

mgmt_strict_v1.source: V1-refined (Agent I/V1 T11); Wave 2 finding replaced bare 'manage' regex (14% precision in SWE JDs)
mgmt_strict_v1.rationale: Narrow pattern: mentor/coach + hiring verbs (not 'being hired') + headcount + performance_review. Dominant contributor is 'mentor' (13.9%->20.8% arshkon->scraped). SNR 5.09 on cross-period move.
mgmt_strict_v1.use_when: measuring whether a SWE JD assigns people-management responsibilities to the incumbent

ai_strict_v1.source: V1-refined (SNR 38.7 vs within-2024 noise). Dropped from v0: agent_bare (66% FP), mcp (57% FP).
ai_strict_v1.rationale: Specific AI-coding-assistant and LLM-infra vocabulary. Zero FPs observed. Use this pattern for any claim with 'AI-as-tool' framing.
ai_strict_v1.use_when: counting postings that mention specific AI coding tools or LLM infrastructure

ai_broad_v1.regex: \b(copilot|cursor|claude|chatgpt|openai api|gpt-?\d+|gemini|codex|llamaindex|langchain|prompt engineering|fine[- ]tuning|rag|vector database|pinecone|huggingface|hugging face|ai|artificial intelligence|ml|machine learning|llm|large language model|generative ai|genai|anthropic)\b
ai_broad_v1.source: V1-refined. Dropped from v0: agent_bare, mcp. Bare 'ai' and 'ml' introduce ambiguity (AIG, chai, etc.) -> 10/50 AMB
ai_broad_v1.rationale: Conservative precision 0.80 (exactly threshold). Most AMB cases are bare 'ai' in compound words; pattern is still usable but broad-AI effect sizes should be reported alongside strict.
ai_broad_v1.use_when: measuring any AI/ML/LLM vocabulary mention (strict+domain). Always pair with strict variant in reports.

ai_tool_v1.source: T22-defined. Subset of ai_strict with codex removed for T23 specificity slicing.
ai_tool_v1.rationale: Specific tools that developers use day-to-day; use for 'specific tools vs generic AI' divergence slice.
ai_tool_v1.use_when: identifying postings that name specific AI coding tools (Copilot/Cursor/Claude/etc.)

ai_domain_v1.regex_loose: \b(machine learning|ml|deep learning|nlp|natural language processing|computer vision|cv|data science|model training|neural network|gradient|transformer|embedding)\b
ai_domain_v1.regex: \b(machine learning|deep learning|natural language processing|computer vision|data science|model training|neural network|transformer architecture|gradient descent|token embedding|nlp(?:\s+engineer|\s+models|\s+pipeline)?)\b
ai_domain_v1.source: T22-defined traditional ML/NLP/CV vocabulary. V1 tightened: bare 'ml'/'cv' acronyms dropped (precision 0.78 on loose); full-name forms and qualified 'nlp' keep 1.00. Use regex (primary) not regex_loose.
ai_domain_v1.rationale: Loose (bare ML/CV) hit 0.78 — below threshold. Tightened to full phrase names and NLP with a verb/noun qualifier; zero FPs observed on 50-sample.

aspiration_v2.regex: \b(nice to have|would be a plus|is a plus|are a plus|big plus|a plus but not required|plus but not required|bonus points|a bonus|is a bonus|preferred(?:\s+qualification)?|preferred but not required|ideally|ideal candidate|would be nice|helpful but not required|we would love|we'd love|exposure to|familiarity with|working knowledge of|nice-to-have|desirable|beneficial)\b
aspiration_v2.source: T22-defined. Requires requirement-context phrase, not bare 'plus' (too noisy in tech/math context).
aspiration_v2.rationale: Hedging language around requirements. One AMB was 'preferred' in non-requirement context. 'familiarity with' / 'exposure to' / 'working knowledge of' are reliable hedges in SWE JDs.
aspiration_v2.use_when: quantifying aspirational / wishful requirement framing per posting; numerator of aspiration ratio

firm_requirement_v2.regex: \b(must have|must be|must possess|you must|required(?!\s+(?:by|to\s+apply))|requirement(?:s)?|minimum (?:qualification|requirement|of|experience|years)|mandatory|essential(?:\s+qualification)?|non[- ]negotiable|strictly required|hard requirement|basic qualification)\b
firm_requirement_v2.source: T22-defined. Negative lookahead on 'required' to exclude 'required to apply/disclose' legal boilerplate.
firm_requirement_v2.rationale: Firm requirement phrasing. Excludes legal 'required to disclose/apply'. 'minimum' qualified to quantity/experience contexts.
firm_requirement_v2.use_when: quantifying strict/hard requirement phrasing; denominator of aspiration ratio

org_scope_v2.source: T22-defined. Mirrors V1-era requirement_breadth scope components.
org_scope_v2.rationale: Organizational-scope cues (stakeholders, cross-functional, roadmap, mentor, etc). 'vision' excludes 'vision care benefits'. 'org/organization' AMB when not qualified by scope verb.
org_scope_v2.use_when: measuring requirement breadth beyond individual contributor; feeds kitchen_sink composite
